members.name: fileStartTimecode
members.description: Get a Timecode object for the timecode start provided by the file, if availble. Returns None if the file doesn't provide it. See Timecode Class.

members.name: metadata
members.description: Dictionary containing all the metadata.

members.name: fileHeight
members.description: Height of the movie, in pixels.

members.name: fileWidth
members.description: Width of the movie, in pixels.

members.name: hasAudio
members.description: True if the movie contains audio.

members.name: hasDecodeErrors
members.description: True if any frames failed to decode, likely due to file corruption. Currently only works on Hap codec files.

members.name: index
members.description: Current movie index.

members.name: indexFraction
members.description: Current movie index expressed as a fraction of total length.

members.name: isFullyPreRead
members.description: True if the movie has pre-read all of its Pre-Read frames and is ready to play. For single images this is true when the image is ready to be shown. When a movie is playing, this member will flip between True and False as its pre-read frames get consumed/refilled. When a movie isn't playing forward, this member can tell you if the movie is in an optimal state to start playing back from its currently selected frame/cued frame.

members.name: isInvalid
members.description: True if the movie has failed to load.

members.name: isLastFrame
members.description: True if the movie is currently showing its last frame.

members.name: isLoopFrame
members.description: True if the movie has just looped and is showing the first frame after a loop.

members.name: isOddField
members.description: True if the current displayed de-interlaced frame is the odd field.

members.name: isOpen
members.description: True if the file has been opened.

members.name: isOpening
members.description: True when the file is opening.

members.name: isFileOpening
members.description: Returns true while the file header is being read.

members.name: isPreloading
members.description: True when the file is preloading.

members.name: lastIndexUploaded
members.description: The index of the if the last frame uploaded to the GPU.

members.name: numHeaders
members.description: Returns the number of key-value pairs in the file's header.

members.name: numImages
members.description: The number of images in the movie.

members.name: numSeconds
members.description: The number of seconds in the movie.

members.name: rate
members.description: The movie sample rate.

members.name: sourceChannels
members.description: A list of the available channels in the file e.g. R, G, B, A for typical color images.

members.name: start
members.description: The start index of the movie.

members.name: trueIndex
members.description: The actual current index of the movie, disregarding trimming and other options.

members.name: trueNumImages
members.description: The actual number of images contained in the movie, not affected by trimming.

members.name: timecode
members.description: Get a Timecode object for the timecode data representation of the true current index of the movie. See Timecode Class.

methods.name: findHeader
methods.description: Returns the value of the header with the given key. This method will return a blank string if the header is not found. *key - The name of the header to search for.

methods.name: getHeader
methods.description: Returns a tuple with the key and value of the header at the given index. This method will throw an error if the index is not valid. The numHeaders member can be used to get the number of valid headers in the file. *index - The index of the header to search for starting with 0.

methods.name: unload
methods.description: Unloads the movie and frees its memory usage. The movie will open again next time it cooks, so make sure nothing is still using it to keep it closed. *cacheMemory - (Keyword, Optional) If True, this will attempt to avoid freeing the GPU memory used to upload/display the movie. This memory can be then re-used by a future movie that is opened, assuming it has the same resolution and codec. This can help avoid stutters that occur during file loading. This isn't nesseary to do when using preload() within a single Movie File In TOP, since preload() automatically try to re-use memory. It can be useful if a different Movie File In TOP will be later opened with the same resolution/codec. Single frame movies and images are always unloaded after they are done opening, except for the final texture used for the TOP.

methods.name: preload
methods.description: Preloads the movie by opening it and pre-reading the first frames. Use the isFullyPreRead member to see if it's ready to play. This is done in a way to minimize impact on the existing playback of the application. If movies are being loaded on startup it's better to just call cook() on the Movie File In TOP instead of preload() since cook() will be more thorough. *index - (Optional) If specified the movie will be opened at the specified frame index. If not, then the movie will be opened at the index specified by its parameters.

_provenance.fact: scraped from the MoviefileinTOP Class wiki page
